summary: Backend-only regression for the 'MT5 = source of truth' refactor + new risk guard-rails + removed real-mode confirmation phrase. Added /app/backend/tests/test_source_of_truth.py with 14 new tests. Full suite: 56/56 PASSED in 46.94s (42 previous + 14 new). All target features verified against LOCAL backend (http://localhost:8001) only — the user's live VPS (EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL) was never called. Local bot restored to demo + disabled at end of run. On Linux backend, MT5 native lib unavailable so state.source is always 'sim' (expected behaviour). New unified /api/bot/state shape verified (state.source, margin, free_margin, account_currency, leverage, config_mode, config_enabled, positions, mt5_status, mt5_account). New metrics fields verified (pnl_today, pnl_week, pnl_month, avg_win, avg_loss, win_loss_ratio, source). MT5 credentials trimming + first-save-without-password rejection + resave-without-password preservation verified. Mode switch to real WITHOUT confirmation_phrase returns 200 as expected. WebSocket snapshot includes state.source + positions.

minor.issue: Cosmetic: 'AttributeError: module bcrypt has no attribute __about__' on passlib import (still present, login works). Pin bcrypt<4 or upgrade passlib to silence. Same as previous iterations.

Naming inconsistency in the review request: the spec mentions POST /api/bot/config/risk but the actual endpoint is PUT /api/bot/risk (which DOES accept the new fields weekly_loss_limit_pct, max_total_drawdown_pct, max_spread_pct). Tests use the correct PUT path. Just flagging in case the main agent wants to expose an alias for clarity.

In real mode with MT5 disconnected on Linux, state.source returns 'sim' (because live_account.is_live=False when mt5 not connected). The PRD distinguishes 'mt5_stale' (connected but no account) from 'sim' (no live mode). Current path is consistent with the code but the UI may want a dedicated 'real_no_mt5' source label so the user understands 'I am in real mode but seeing the simulator'. Cosmetic / UX, not a bug.
Token still in query string ?token=<JWT> (logged by uvicorn). Optional hardening, same as iteration 2.

action_items: Nothing blocking — all 56 backend tests green.
Optional: pin bcrypt<4 in /app/backend/requirements.txt to remove passlib warning.
Optional UX: differentiate 'sim' vs 'real_no_mt5' in state.source so the UI can clearly tell the user 'You are in real mode but MT5 is not connected — values shown come from the simulator'.
Optional: split /app/backend/server.py (now 1100 lines) into routers/{auth,bot,trades,mt5,ws,costs,backtest}.py — same recommendation since iteration 1.
Optional: extract _build_snapshot_data() duplication in _send_snapshot vs _ws_broadcast_loop (DRY — flagged iteration 2).
Optional: harden WS auth (avoid token in query string).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1100 lines (1.5x the 700-line threshold). Strongly recommend splitting into routers/.

live_account.resolve_state: '_daily_cache' is a module-level dict mutated without a lock — fine in single-process uvicorn but would break under multi-worker deployment. Use asyncio.Lock or per-user caching keyed by user id.

live_account.resolve_state: in real mode when mt5 returns no account, source='mt5_stale' but the other fields (balance/equity) keep the LAST sim values from state.model_dump(); these may mislead the UI. Consider returning None or zeroing those fields, or attaching a 'last_seen_at' timestamp.

_resolve_trades treats mode='real' specially but if the user requests mode='demo' explicitly while MT5 is connected and bot is in 'real' mode, demo trades are correctly returned. Good. However, when mode is None and cfg.mode='real' but MT5 disconnected, the helper falls back to ALL trades (no mode filter) instead of cfg.mode='real' trades. Consider always filtering by cfg.mode when mode is None.
services/mt5_broker.disconnect() does not reset self.mode='unavailable' nor clear last_error / last_heartbeat (flagged iteration 2 — still present).
WebSocket /api/ws is registered on the raw `app` rather than the `api` router (cosmetic, works because the path is hard-coded).

models.RiskConfig.max_spread_pct default 0.1% is quite tight for crypto symbols like BTCUSD where intraday spread often exceeds 0.1%. Consider a per-symbol override or a higher default for the crypto bucket.

models._clean_str strips wrapping single/double quotes — fine for human typos but could silently break a legitimate broker password that contains leading/trailing quotes. Acceptable trade-off but worth documenting in the API doc.

success_rate.frontend: not tested (backend-only request, explicit interdiction on VPS)

seed_data_creation: Tests overwrite admin MT5 credentials twice (login='123' and login='987654' with 'PreservedPwd!'). Each TestMT5CredentialsTrimMerge test creates one ephemeral test_mt5_trim_<uuid>@example.com user (no cleanup). Test data is harmless and confined to the local Mongo (DB_NAME=trading_bot). Local bot is forcibly reset to mode=demo + enabled=false in the final teardown test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is healthy: 56/56. Local Mongo (trading_bot) has been mutated by tests: admin user now has MT5 credentials login='987654', server='ICMarkets-Live', path='C:\\Program Files\\MetaTrader 5\\terminal64.exe' (encrypted) — re-run TestMT5 in test_backend_api.py if you need them reset. Bot is left in mode=demo + enabled=false. Admin: admin@trading.bot / Trading2025!. Local backend URL: http://localhost:8001 (ingress: https://autotrader-hub-28.preview.emergentagent.com/api). NEVER call EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env — that points to the user's REAL trading VPS. On Linux, MT5 native lib unavailable so state.source is always 'sim' and mt5_status.connected=false — that is expected.
rca_of_issue: No bug discovered this iteration. The refactor 'MT5 = source of truth' is correctly implemented: in demo mode all account data comes from BotState + market simulator (source='sim'); in real mode with MT5 connected, balance/equity/margin/free_margin/positions come from mt5_connector.get_account_info/get_positions (source='mt5'); when in real mode but MT5 unreachable, the system falls back to the sim values (source='sim') so the UI never breaks. The confirmation phrase gate has been removed from /api/bot/mode (paper_validation_enabled=False by default). New risk fields (weekly_loss_limit_pct, max_total_drawdown_pct, max_spread_pct) are wired through RiskConfig -> persisted via PUT /api/bot/risk -> consumed in bot_runner.py guard-rails.
